summary_en: Simplified stock exchange: SINANEN to acquire E-Smart Energy as wholly-owned subsidiary via issuance of 28,000 SINANEN shares (exchange ratio 1:28). Effective June 30, 2026. No shareholder vote required at SINANEN. Rationale: integration of LP gas sales business to strengthen energy operations.
title_en: Notice Regarding the Acquisition of E-Smart Energy Co., Ltd. as a Wholly-Owned Subsidiary through Simplified Stock Exchange
translation_en_brief: SINANEN Holdings announced on May 29, 2026 that its board has approved a simplified stock exchange to acquire E-Smart Energy Co., Ltd. as a wholly-owned subsidiary. Under the exchange, SINANEN will issue 28,000 of its ordinary shares at a ratio of 1:28 (one E-Smart Energy share exchanges for 28 SINANEN shares). The exchange will take effect on June 30, 2026. E-Smart Energy's shareholders will vote on the stock exchange contract at a meeting held May 29, 2026; however, SINANEN will not require shareholder approval because the transaction qualifies as a simplified stock exchange under the Companies Act (target's net assets are less than 10% and sales less than 3% of SINANEN's corresponding figures). SINANEN plans to use 189,901 treasury shares currently held to fulfill the 28,000-share obligation. The rationale is that full integration of E-Smart Energy, which will inherit LP gas sales operations from parent company Ecolog on June 29, 2026, will deepen coordination with SINANAN's existing energy business subsidiary and strengthen sales capabilities.
